component.name: Doubao Murmur
豆包语音输入
component.summary: 使用豆包语音识别的语音输入工具

description.p: Doubao Murmur is a voice-to-text input tool that uses Doubao's ASR (Automatic Speech Recognition) service to convert speech to text in real-time. It works as a global overlay with a push-to-talk button, making it easy to dictate text into any application.
豆包语音输入是一款语音转文字工具，使用豆包的 ASR（自动语音识别）服务 实时将语音转换为文字。它以全局悬浮窗的形式工作，提供一键录音按钮， 可以轻松地将文字输入到任何应用中。

On-screen keyboard one-handed mode: widen from 22% to 35% of the screen so keys are large enough to tap (~6mm instead of ~4mm), and fix a keyboard reopened directly in a one-handed/split mode keeping a stale saved width and missing its compact styling. Width is now always derived from the current mode and monitor.

Recording overlay: wrap transcription text properly. Chinese has no spaces, so the default word-wrap found no break points and the text overflowed into a head-truncating ellipsis pinned at two lines. The overlay now wraps between characters and grows in height up to five lines before the oldest words scroll off.

Change the on-screen keyboard's global hotkey to Ctrl+Super+Shift (pure modifiers). The old Alt+Shift+F10+F11 chord leaked F-key escape sequences into focused terminals; modifier-only keys emit no characters, so nothing leaks.
On-screen keyboard: make key presses reliable on the touchscreen (fire on the native button click, drop the gesture/poll path that dropped touch-release events and could deadlock the keyboard)
Add an on-screen touch keyboard: full/split/one-handed layout modes, sticky modifiers, symbols layer, drag & resize, tray toggle, and the Alt+Shift+F10+F11 global hotkey
Add system tray icon (StatusNotifierItem) with status menu
